prerequisites: An active EZOfficeInventory account whose subdomain you know. The subdomain is the company name chosen at signup and is visible in the URL when you are logged in (for example the SUBDOMAIN in https://SUBDOMAIN.ezofficeinventory.com).
API access enabled for the company. The API feature is disabled by default and must be turned on in Settings before any access token can be used.
Account owner access. Updating API settings and generating or regenerating the access token requires the account owner to be logged in.
A replication start date to bound the initial sync (the start_date config field).

authMethods.name: API access token (token header)
authMethods.summary: EZOfficeInventory uses a single company access token for authentication, which maps to this connector's api_key secret field. Every request is authenticated by sending the token in an HTTP header named token (for example -H "token:<ACCESS_TOKEN>"), and all calls must be made over HTTPS. Keep the token secret and regenerate it if it is compromised.

setupSteps.title: Enable the API and generate an access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to EZOfficeInventory as the account owner and go to Settings. Enable the API for the company (it is disabled by default), then generate an access token. Copy the token; this value is the connector's api_key. Treat it as a secret and regenerate it in the same place if it is ever exposed.

setupSteps.title: Identify your subdomain
setupSteps.body: Find your account subdomain, which is the company name used at signup and appears in your URL when logged in. Requests are made against https://<subdomain>.ezofficeinventory.com, so enter only the subdomain portion in the subdomain config field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: In the connector configuration, set api_key to the access token from step 1, set subdomain to your account subdomain from step 2, and set start_date to the earliest date from which data should be synced. All three fields are required.

setupSteps.title: Note rate limits
setupSteps.body: EZOfficeInventory applies a fair-use limit of 60 API requests per minute for paying clients, and in-trial accounts are limited to roughly 1,000 requests per day. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests; remaining quota is reported via the X-Rate-Limit and X-Rate-Limit-Remaining response headers.
